meta.name: Cost Delta Defaults
meta.label: Cost Delta Defaults
meta.subtitle: Default cost deltas and adjustment factors used by SSA estimators and planners

source.name: src/catalogs/estimators/cost_delta_defaults.catalog.json
notes: Deterministic default multipliers/deltas used by SSA estimators when exact prices are unknown.
Used by EstimateEngine/ProvisioningTargetEngine as a fallback until user prices or store data exist.
All values are conservative and intended to be user-overridable.

items.label: Source Baseline Weights
items.notes: Relative cost deltas compared to baseline source.grocery. Negative means cheaper than baseline, positive means more expensive.

notes: Garden and animals assume sunk costs are not fully amortized here; treat as marginal food cost delta.
Restaurant includes labor/overhead; use only for 'eat out' comparisons.
Engines may combine this with readiness and quality adjustments.

items.label: Readiness Baseline Weights
items.notes: Relative cost multipliers by readiness/processing level. Intended to model convenience costs.

notes: Whole/raw are typically cheaper but require more labor/time.
Prepared/frozen prepared tend to cost more due to processing and packaging.

items.label: Quality Baseline Weights
items.notes: Relative cost multipliers by quality tier.

notes: Premium/organic/grass-fed deltas vary widely by region; treat as placeholders.
Engines should prefer user price history when available.

items.label: Waste & Shrink Defaults
items.notes: Default waste/shrink percentages by category. Used to inflate purchase quantities and true-cost estimates.

notes: Produce/herbs waste includes spoilage and trim.
Meat waste includes bone/trim depending on cut; butchery can shift this significantly.

items.label: Cooking Energy Defaults
items.notes: Rough energy-cost deltas by cooking method, for comparing scratch vs convenience or method substitutions. Units are USD per hour-equivalent.

notes: These are directional; engines should not treat as bill-accurate unless user energy rates are known.
Useful for tradeoff explanations: 'slow cooker is lower energy per hour than oven'.

items.label: Labor Time Value Defaults
items.notes: Default imputed value of time used for 'true cost' comparisons (scratch vs convenience).

notes: Set to 'none' for pure cash-outlay mode.
Engines can compute totalCost = cashCost + laborHours * usdPerHour when enabled.

items.label: Bulk Purchase Discount Defaults
items.notes: Default discount multipliers based on bulk purchase size. Intended for storehouse planning and provisioning targets.

notes: Discounts vary by item; apply selectively (staples > perishables).
Combine with storage constraints and waste buffers.

items.label: Substitution Penalties/Bonuses
items.notes: Default score/cost nudges when swapping components. Used by substitution engines when multiple candidates exist.

notes: ScoreAdjust is used for ranking when costs are equal/unknown.
costDeltaPct is used to approximate cash differences when no prices exist.

items.label: Category Multipliers (Relative)
items.notes: Relative cost multipliers by broad category. Helps estimate baskets from calories/macros when item-level pricing is missing.

notes: Meat/seafood cost multipliers reflect common US pricing; user prices should override.
